mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by higher 20-day returns than unchanged or rising-rate periods.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates labor-market deterioration and impending earnings weakness, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is rising faster than in the prior quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent pickup in RSAFS and HOUST signals stronger household and housing demand, improving growth expectations and supporting equity prices over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when one series improves but the other remains flat, because the demand impulse is not broad enough.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST do not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial supply conditions and stronger cyclical activity, which tends to lift near-term earnings expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is confirmed by positive monthly industrial production momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not associated with positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign reverses in more than half of rolling annual subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates are negative and the market interprets policy as behind the curve, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is invalid when inflation is falling sharply because the real-rate gap is closing.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rising and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL inflation rate
regime_hint: inflation surprise and policy lag regime
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive inflation-real rate gaps do not precede lower 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 signals stronger aggregate output and earnings capacity, which should support equity prices over the following quarter. The effect is a slow-moving growth confirmation signal, not a short-lived trading impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 growth does not align with higher 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward tests, or if growth periods underperform contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a broadening labor-market slowdown that reduces expected consumer income and corporate sales, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism requires both payroll loss and unemployment deterioration to be present.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll declines and unemployment increases fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A weak retail-sales and housing combination, marked by falling RSAFS with falling HOUST, signals broad demand contraction and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The pattern is a stronger bearish signal than either series alone because it spans consumption and housing.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines and HOUST declines over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in retail sales and housing starts do not precede lower 60-day SPX returns, or if positive 60-day returns occur in most triggered observations.
